member.name: Gareth Thomas
member.synopsis: Gareth Thomas is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Harrow West , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 1997.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Crown Court: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of reclassification of certain offences to summary only on reducing the backlog in the Crown Courts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Part 1 of the Independent Review of the Criminal Courts considered the potential impact of reclassifying certain triable either way offences as summary only offences. Sir Brian Leveson’s analysis indicated that reclassification, together with restricting ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Criminal Proceedings: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, What recent progress he has made on the implementation of £34 million for criminal advocates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have been working to ensure that the up to £34 million per year investment in criminal legal aid advocate fees delivers the greatest possible impact and lasting benefit. It will support the long-term sustainability of the advocate profession while alig...

writtenQuestions.heading: Courts: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps his Department is taking to ensure court proceedings are not delayed by the late arrival of prisoners.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are taking a cross-system approach to improving the timely delivery of prisoners to court. Lord Timpson and I have established a Prisoner Delivery Oversight Board, bringing together senior leaders from across the Criminal Justice System to monitor perf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Co-operative and Community Benefit Societies Act 2014
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what discussions her Department have had on the scope, conduct or progress of its review of the Co-operative and Community Benefit Societies Act with the Law Commission.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is keen to ensure that the law governing co-operatives and community benefit societies is clear and supports their growth. That is why we are funding the Law Commission’s independent review of the Co-operative and Community Benefit Societie...

writtenQuestions.heading: Discretionary Trusts: Property
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the number of properties placed in Family Protection Trusts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Treasury has not made any estimate of the number of properties placed in Family Protection Trusts. Since 1 September 2022 most types of UK express trusts have been required to register with HMRC through the Trust Registration Service (TRS). However, t...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health and Care Professions Council: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that the Health and Care Professions Council meets the Professional Standards Authority Standard 15 on the time taken to conclude cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government takes the performance of the health and care professional regulators, including the Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC), very seriously. On 27 June 2025, the Professional Standards Authority for Health and Social Care (PSA) published...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Professions: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will consider standardising the disciplinary sanctions available to healthcare regulators at the closure of cases.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to modernising the regulation of all healthcare professionals in the United Kingdom. As a first step, on 24 March 2026, we published our Reforming the General Medical Council legislative framework consultation, which sets out p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Professions: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of implementing the recommendation for the creation of a single assurance body proposed by the Professional Standards Authority in its report R
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has no plans to implement the Professional Standards Authority for Health and Social Care’s proposal to create a single assurance body for all healthcare professionals, as set out in its 2016 Regulation Rethought report.The Government is co...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The regulators of registered healthcare professionals, including the General Medical Council (GMC) and the Health and Care Professions Council (HCPC), are independent of the Government, as they are directly accountable to Parliament and responsible for op...

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Secondment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what programmes are available to encourage businesses to provide industrial secondments to university lecturers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Economic growth is the central mission of this government and is vital to give the next generation the opportunities to thrive.The government encourages work experience through various initiatives spanning various departments. The Department for Business ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Students: Work Experience
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assistance is being provided to businesses to help them offer work placements to undergraduates.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Economic growth is the central mission of this government and is vital to give the next generation the opportunities to thrive.The government encourages work experience through various initiatives spanning various departments. The Department for Business ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prime Minister: Credit Unions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government will require No 10 to offer payroll deductions to all its employees to enable them to join a credit union.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office and its agencies do not offer payroll deductions to staff in order to join a credit union.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Credit Unions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether they will require their department and agencies to offer payroll deductions to all employees to enable them to join a credit union.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office and its agencies do not offer payroll deductions to staff in order to join a credit union.

writtenQuestions.heading: Teachers: Credit Unions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of encouraging education employers to promote membership of a credit union to teachers and non-teaching staff.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is a strong supporter of the mutual sector, including credit unions. The department is not the employer of any school staff, and it would therefore be inappropriate to encourage or recommend membership to specific financial products or inst...

writtenQuestions.heading: Northwick Park Hospital: Accident and Emergency Departments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many people attended Northwick Park Hospital A&E Department in each of the last 6 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England began publishing data on accident and emergency attendances at the site-level from November 2025, and therefore only two months of data are currently available. The following table shows the number of type 1 and 2 accident and emergency attend...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Languages: GCSE
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if she will hold discussions with exam boards on offering GCSEs in Tamil.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions about which languages to offer at GCSE in England are taken by four independent awarding organisations – AQA, OCR, Pearson Edexcel and WJEC – rather than by central government. These organisations have the freedom to create a Tamil GCSE based on...
